title: No valid high-error found for MultiDimFit!

posts.name: Khawla Jaffel
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
I am looking at the nuisance parameter impacts using the Asimov dataset.
In some categories of signal regions, I get the following error from a few mass points.
Have POIs: ['r']
<<< Combine >>>
>>> random number generator seed is 123456
Options for Robust Minimizer ::
Tolerance  0.1
Strategy   0
Type,Algo  Minuit2,Migrad
>>> method used is MultiDimFit
SimNLL created with 1 channels, 0 generic constraints, 87 fast gaussian constraints, 0 fast poisson constraints, 0 fast group constraints,
Error: closed range without finding crossing.

--- MultiDimFit ---
best fit parameter values and profile-likelihood uncertainties:
Warning - No valid high-error found, will report difference to maximum of range for : r
r :    +0.000   -0.030/+20.000 (68%)
Done in 0.03 min (cpu), 0.05 min (real)
>> combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --robustFit 1 -m 125 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root

The fit always reports the --rMax I set in the command even if I go up to 99999.
These are the commands I am using;
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --robustFit 1 --doFits --parallel 60
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 -o impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.json

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.pdf (29.5 KB)
Now I tried different things in Combine to make this work like;
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=999999999 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_analytic --cminDefaultMinimizerPrecision 1E-12 --robustHesse=1 but I still get the same error.
Surprisingly I managed by just simply adding --expectSignal 1 instead of --expectSignal 0 to the 3 commands above, and here is the result of the test.
Have POIs: ['r']
<<< Combine >>>
>>> random number generator seed is 123456
Options for Robust Minimizer ::
Tolerance  0.1
Strategy   0
Type,Algo  Minuit2,Migrad
>>> method used is MultiDimFit
SimNLL created with 1 channels, 0 generic constraints, 87 fast gaussian constraints, 0 fast poisson constraints, 0 fast group constraints,

--- MultiDimFit ---
best fit parameter values and profile-likelihood uncertainties:
r :    +1.000   -0.182/+0.287 (68%)
Done in 0.02 min (cpu), 0.05 min (real)
>> combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -t -1 --expectSignal 1 --robustFit 1 -m 125 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts of this test:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal1_asimovdataset.pdf (29.2 KB)
I wonder if anyone can help me understand why the fit works with --expectSignal 1 and not --expectSignal 0?
I am also attaching the datacard:
HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0.dat (14.8 KB)
Many thanks in advance for any feedback or comments.
Cheers,
Khawla
posts.cooked_html:
Dear combine experts,

I am looking at the nuisance parameter impacts using the Asimov dataset.
In some categories of signal regions, I get the following error from a few mass points.

Have POIs: ['r']
<<< Combine >>>

Options for Robust Minimizer ::

    Strategy   0
    Type,Algo  Minuit2,Migrad

Error: closed range without finding crossing.

--- MultiDimFit ---
best fit parameter values and profile-likelihood uncertainties:
Warning - No valid high-error found, will report difference to maximum of range for : r
    r :    +0.000   -0.030/+20.000 (68%)
Done in 0.03 min (cpu), 0.05 min (real)
>> combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --robustFit 1 -m 125 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root

The fit always reports the --rMax I set in the command even if I go up to 99999.
These are the commands I am using;

combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --robustFit 1 --doFits --parallel 60
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 -o impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.json

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.pdf (29.5 KB)

Now I tried different things in Combine to make this work like;
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=999999999 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_analytic --cminDefaultMinimizerPrecision 1E-12 --robustHesse=1 but I still get the same error.
Surprisingly I managed by just simply adding --expectSignal 1 instead of --expectSignal 0 to the 3 commands above, and here is the result of the test.

Have POIs: ['r']
<<< Combine >>>
>>> random number generator seed is 123456
Options for Robust Minimizer ::
    Tolerance  0.1
    Strategy   0
    Type,Algo  Minuit2,Migrad
>>> method used is MultiDimFit
SimNLL created with 1 channels, 0 generic constraints, 87 fast gaussian constraints, 0 fast poisson constraints, 0 fast group constraints,

--- MultiDimFit ---
best fit parameter values and profile-likelihood uncertainties:
    r :    +1.000   -0.182/+0.287 (68%)
Done in 0.02 min (cpu), 0.05 min (real)
>> combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -t -1 --expectSignal 1 --robustFit 1 -m 125 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts of this test:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal1_asimovdataset.pdf (29.2 KB)

I wonder if anyone can help me understand why the fit works with --expectSignal 1 and not --expectSignal 0?

I am also attaching the datacard:
HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0.dat (14.8 KB)

Many thanks in advance for any feedback or comments.

Cheers,
Khawla

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Dear Khawla,
What if you try to set rMax to something smaller, for example 1 or 2?
Best,
Adinda

posts.text:

Thank you for looking into this. Indeed, I tried --rMax 1 and --rMax 2  and both worked.

posts.name: Mintu Kumar
posts.text:
Hi Adinda,
I am also getting simmilar problem. In my case I am trying to run the parametric fits and r is a POI for me. For my case the mean and sigma of the distribution are POI for me. I am trying to run. the following command
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace_top_Mass_1725_shape_comb_para.root -m 172.5 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 -n _M1725_InitialFit --redefineSignalPOIs sigmaG,mean  --setParameters mean=5.1,r=1,sigmaG=0.15 --freezeParameters r --X-rtd ADDNLL_CBNLL=0
I have pested the output of the command in the end of the post. I can see there are two issues, the first one is the max and min range of the POI are not given. I like to know how to do that. Second is [ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing! and I don’t how can fix this as well. I have attached my datacard here:
datacard_top_shape_comb_para.txt (6.9 KB)
Can you please suggest something which can fix this issue.
Thanks in advance.
Mintu Kumar
[mikumar@lxplus9110 Combine_Run2]$ combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace_top_Mass_1725_shape_comb_para.root -m 172.5 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 -n _M1725_InitialFit --redefineSignalPOIs sigmaG,mean  --setParameters mean=5.1,r=1,sigmaG=0.15 --freezeParameters r --X-rtd ADDNLL_CBNLL=0
Have POIs: [‘sigmaG’, ‘mean’]

combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit__M1725_InitialFit --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs sigmaG,mean --robustFit 1 --freezeParameters r --X-rtd ADDNLL_CBNLL=0 -m 172.5 -d workspace_top_Mass_1725_shape_comb_para.root --setParameters mean=5.1,r=1,sigmaG=0.15

<<< v10.0.2 >>>

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Dear Mintu,
Do you have any insight about where do you expect the crossing to be? It could be that the range you allow for your POIs is too large or too small.
If you have no clue, you can use —algo grid to see the actual shape of the likelihood function and extrapolate where the crossings should be.
Once you have an idea about what range is reasonable as suggested by Adinda, you can use the command —setPatamerRanges to adjust the ranges of mean and sigma and see if it helps.
Cheers,
Giacomo

combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --robustFit 1 --doFits --parallel 60
combineTool.py -M Impacts --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root -m 125 -n HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 -o impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.json

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal0_asimovdataset.pdf (29.5 KB)
Now I tried different things in Combine to make this work like;
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_MaxCalls=999999999 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_analytic --cminDefaultMinimizerPrecision 1E-12 --robustHesse=1 but I still get the same error.
Surprisingly I managed by just simply adding --expectSignal 1 instead of --expectSignal 0 to the 3 commands above, and here is the result of the test.
Have POIs: ['r']
<<< Combine >>>
>>> random number generator seed is 123456
Options for Robust Minimizer ::
Tolerance  0.1
Strategy   0
Type,Algo  Minuit2,Migrad
>>> method used is MultiDimFit
SimNLL created with 1 channels, 0 generic constraints, 87 fast gaussian constraints, 0 fast poisson constraints, 0 fast group constraints,

--- MultiDimFit ---
best fit parameter values and profile-likelihood uncertainties:
r :    +1.000   -0.182/+0.287 (68%)
Done in 0.02 min (cpu), 0.05 min (real)
>> combine -M MultiDimFit -n _initialFit_HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0 --algo singles --redefineSignalPOIs r --rMin -20 --rMax 20 -t -1 --expectSignal 1 --robustFit 1 -m 125 -d HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_combine_workspace.root

Please also take a look at the pulls/impacts of this test:
impacts__HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0_expectSignal1_asimovdataset.pdf (29.2 KB)
I wonder if anyone can help me understand why the fit works with --expectSignal 1 and not --expectSignal 0?
I am also attaching the datacard:
HToZATo2L2B_gg_fusion_nb2_resolved_ElEl_dnn_MH_500.0_MA_300.0.dat (14.8 KB)
Many thanks in advance for any feedback or comments.
Cheers,
Khawla

posts.cooked_html:
Dear Mintu,

Do you have any insight about where do you expect the crossing to be? It could be that the range you allow for your POIs is too large or too small.
If you have no clue, you can use —algo grid to see the actual shape of the likelihood function and extrapolate where the crossings should be.
Once you have an idea about what range is reasonable as suggested by Adinda, you can use the command —setPatamerRanges to adjust the ranges of mean and sigma and see if it helps.

Cheers,
Giacomo

On 29 Sep 2024, at 12:34, Mintu Kumar via CMS talk noreply-cms-talk@cern.ch wrote:

Mintu Kumar mikumar
September 29

Hi Adinda,
I am also getting simmilar problem. In my case I am trying to run the parametric fits and r is a POI for me. For my case the mean and sigma of the distribution are POI for me. I am trying to run. the following command
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d workspace_top_Mass_1725_shape_comb_para.root -m 172.5 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 -n _M1725_InitialFit --redefineSignalPOIs sigmaG,mean --setParameters mean=5.1,r=1,sigmaG=0.15 --freezeParameters r --X-rtd ADDNLL_CBNLL=0

I have pested the output of the command in the end of the post. I can see there are two issues, the first one is the max and min range of the POI are not given. I like to know how to do that. Second is [ERROR] Closed range without finding crossing! and I don’t how can fix this as well. I have attached my datacard here:
datacard_top_shape_comb_para.txt (6.9 KB)

Can you please suggest something which can fix this issue.

Thanks in advance.
Mintu Kumar

Thanks,
Mintu Kumar
